case_title: NICOLE WILSON et al VS. OE SERVICES, LLC DBA OPEN ENGLISH LLC, A FLORIDA et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY DEFENDANT MORENO, NICOLETTE HEARING SET FOR AUG-13-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
DECLARATION OF GARY K BRUCKER JR IN SUPPORT OF PLTFS OPPOSITION TO NICOLETTE MORENO'S MOTION TO QUASH FILED BY PLAINTIFF WILSON, NICOLE ROSETTA STONE, LTD, A VIRGINA CORPORATION
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-17-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NICOLETTE MORENO AND OE SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFFS PLEAD SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ESTABLISH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS OF LABOR CODE 970, INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, AND BREACH OF CONTRACT. PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS WITH PARTICULARITY AS TO THE "WHO, WHAT, WHEN, TO WHOM, AND BY MEANS" OF DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS. PLAINTIFFS FRAUD CLAIMS ARE ACTIONABLE AS PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANTS KNEW THE PROMISES ABOUT A PROMOTION TO VICE PRESIDENT WERE FALSE AT THE TIME THEY WERE MADE. (SEE COMPLAINT 34, 42, 48; LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT, 12 CAL.4TH 631, 638-39 (1996).) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS TO DEFENDANT OE SERVICES, LLC, AS PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ESTABLISH STANDING UNDER THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW. (COMPLAINT, 25, 60-63.) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION REGARDING DEFENDANT MORENO IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS PLAINTIFFS CONCEDE THE CLAIM IS NOT MERITORIOUS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GARY BRUKER JR., ESQ., 619.595.8062, FOR PLAINTIFFS NICOLE WILSON AND ROSETTA STONE, LTD.
MICHAEL LANE, ESQ., 916.442.1111 X3006, FOR DEFENDANTS NICOLETTE MORENO AND OE SERVICES, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS NICOLETTE MORENO AND OE SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' demurrer to the second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action is overruled. Plaintiffs plead sufficient facts to establish causes of action for violations of Labor Code 970, intentional and negligent misrepresentation, and breach of contract. Plaintiffs allege sufficient facts with particularity as to the "who, what, when, to whom, and by means" of Defendants' alleged misrepresentations. Plaintiffs fraud claims are actionable as Plaintiffs allege that Defendants knew the promises about a promotion to Vice President were false at the time they were made. (See Complaint 34, 42, 48; Lazar v. Superior Court, 12 Cal.4th 631, 638-39 (1996).) Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action is overruled as to Defendant OE Services, LLC, as Plaintiffs allege sufficient facts to establish standing under the Unfair Competition Law. (Complaint, 25, 60-63.) Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action regarding Defendant Moreno is sustained without leave to amend as Plaintiffs concede the claim is not meritorious. Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Continued To Oct-10-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Lane, Esq. of Greenberg Traurig, LLP, Tel. 916.442.1111 ext.3006, for defendant and moving party;
Gary K. Brucker, Jr., Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge, LLP, Tel. 619.595.8062, for plaintiff and opposing party.

DEFENDANT NICOLETTE MORENO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to October 10, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Motion To Stay Or Dismiss
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Lane, Esq. of Greenberg Traurig, LLP, Tel. 916.442.1111 ext.3006, for defendant and moving party;
Gary K. Brucker, Jr., Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge, LLP, Tel. 619.595.8062, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows on DEFENDANT NICOLETTE MORENO's Motion To Stay Or Dismiss. Motion to Stay is denied for reasons stated on the record. The request for judicial notice is granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Lane, Esq. of Greenberg Traurig, LLP, Tel. 916.442.1111 ext.3006, for defendant and moving party;
Gary K. Brucker, Jr., Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge, LLP, Tel. 619.595.8062, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court denies DEFENDANT NICOLETTE MORENO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. There is specific jurisdiction over the defendant. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
